app.name: LibreClinica MUW
app.tagline: Clinical study data platform of the Ophthalmology Department

investigator.subjectMatrixDesc: Site-scoped subject list with per-event status. Search, filter, schedule new event, sign subject.
investigator.addSubjectDesc: Enrol a study subject. PHI-aware validation, gender selector, three save modes.
investigator.scheduleVisitDesc: Plan the next visit for an enrolled subject. Pick the subject in the matrix and choose the event definition + date.
investigator.signQueueDesc: Subjects with completed events ready for e-signature. 21 CFR Part 11 §11.50 attestation.
investigator.todaysCrfsDesc: Subjects with events in data-entry-started state or scheduled for today.

monitor.sdvDesc: Completed CRFs across all subjects with SDV requirement. Bulk-verify, inline Add Query.
monitor.openQueriesDesc: Cross-subject query + annotation list with status / type / assigned-to-me filters.
monitor.auditLogDesc: Gapless timeline with diff cards on reason-for-change. Filter by actor / event type / subject.

dataManager.buildStudyDesc: Seven-task setup tracker with progress bar and per-task status.
dataManager.manageUsersDesc: Per-user role + site + auth method. Pending invites badge.
dataManager.importCrfDesc: Multi-step wizard: Upload → Map → Preview & resolve → Commit.
dataManager.rulesDesc: Validation + workflow rules attached to event-CRFs.

administrator.manageUsersDesc: User accounts, role bindings per study, pending invites, password resets.
administrator.editStudyDesc: Study name, sponsor, protocol number, summary, dates.
administrator.createStudyDesc: Provision a top-level study. You are auto-bound as study coordinator.
administrator.sitesDesc: Site identity + activation + per-site user roles.
administrator.auditLogDesc: Platform audit trail with diff cards on every change.

home.switchStudyDesc: Pick another study you are assigned to. Only your bound studies are shown.

subjectMatrix.title: Subject Matrix
subjectMatrix.empty: No subjects match the current filter.
subjectMatrix.scheduleHint: Pick a subject below to schedule the next visit.

addSubject.title: Add Study Subject
addSubject.intro: Once saved, the subject's identifying fields cannot be changed without contacting the Study Coordinator. Do not include personally identifying information in Secondary ID.

helper.subjectId: The identifier used in source documents. Cannot be edited after save.

addSubject.phiWarning: Never include name, hospital ID, social security number, or any other identifying data.
addSubject.cancelLink: Cancel and return to Subject Matrix
addSubject.modesHelp: Save modes: "Schedule first event" opens the subject view (event scheduler lands in a later sub-phase). "Add next" keeps you here with a clean form. "Finish" returns to the Subject Matrix.

sdv.title: Source Data Verification
sdv.subTrail: All sites · view by Event CRF
sdv.empty: No CRFs match the current filter.
sdv.selectAllAriaLabel: Select all verifiable CRFs in the current view

unverify.title: Roll back verification?
unverify.intro: The SDV stamp is cleared and the CRF returns to the verification queue. The action is recorded in the audit trail with the reason you provide.
unverify.unknownError: Unknown error while rolling back verification.

confirm.title: Mark as verified?
confirm.body: All {count} selected CRFs will be flipped to Verified (SDV). The action is recorded in the audit log.
confirm.note: If data on a verified CRF is later changed, its SDV status resets to Pending automatically.
confirm.auditNote: Audit trail entry will reference monitor_demo + the timestamp.

query.title: Add Note
query.descriptionPlaceholder: What needs clarifying? Reference the source document if relevant…
query.auditTrailTell: Audit trail entry will reference: monitor_demo · timestamp

notes.title: Notes & Discrepancies
notes.empty: No discrepancies match the current filter.

auditLog.title: Study Audit Log
auditLog.intro: Every data change, sign-off, SDV event, query, and administrative action. Immutable. Filters narrow the view; nothing is ever hidden.
auditLog.empty: No events match the current filter.

action.reopenConfirm: Reopen this CRF for editing? Previously signed-off data will be editable again. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

buildStudy.title: Build Study
buildStudy.intro: Seven setup tasks. Complete them in any order; each card deep-links to its workflow when the supporting view ships.

statusDialog.title: Move study to {target}?
statusDialog.intro: The transition is recorded in the audit trail and cascades to all child sites. Locked / Frozen transitions require a reason for the audit log.
statusDialog.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. GCP-mandated lock after database lock review
statusDialog.reasonRequired: Reason is required for Locked / Frozen transitions.

create-study.title: Create study
create-study.description: Study metadata, version, OID, sponsor + protocol number.

crf.title: Design CRFs
crf.description: Form library: item grids, response sets, repeating groups.

events.title: Define events
events.description: Visit schedule with per-event CRF assignment + SDV requirement.

rules.title: Validation rules
rules.description: Field-level and cross-form predicates with action types.

users.title: Users
users.description: Per-site Investigators, Monitors, Data Managers, CRCs.

groupClasses.title: Subject groups
groupClasses.intro: Define how subjects are classified — randomisation arms, family relationships, demographic strata, or ad-hoc groupings. Each class owns a set of groups subjects can be assigned to.
groupClasses.disableConfirm: Remove group class "{name}"? Subjects already classified into its groups stay in the audit trail; new classifications use the remaining classes. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

rules.title: Rules
rules.intro: View what validation, discrepancy-note, show/hide, and notification rules attach to this study. Pick a target on the left to see the attached rules + actions. Editing rules still requires the legacy XML import workflow — inline authoring is queued in a follow-up slice.
rules.empty: No rules defined on this study yet.
rules.searchPlaceholder: Filter by target OID, CRF, rule name, expression…
rules.pickPrompt: Pick a rule_set on the left to see its attached rules + actions.

detail.runLogEmpty: No fire history for this rule set yet.

test.heading: Test rule
test.intro: Parse and evaluate an expression against optional mock values. Useful for sanity-checking range checks and boolean composition before they go into a rule. Variable references against the live study scope are not supported by this surface; use the legacy Test Rule screen for that.

actionEdit.title: Edit action
actionEdit.notEditable: Inline edit not supported for {type}. Delete + recreate via the wizard.

confirm.disableRuleSet: Disable rule set on {target}? Existing fire history stays in the audit trail; the rules stop evaluating until restored.
confirm.disableRule: Disable rule {ruleOid} on this rule set? The other rules attached to the same target stay active.

saveError.timeRequired: Time is required when scheduling is on.

create.heading: Author a new rule
create.actionTypeNote: Inline create supports Discrepancy / Email / Show / Hide only. Use XML import for other action types.

field.oidHint: Uppercase letters, digits, underscores. Must start with a letter. e.g. RUL_BP_HIGH
field.name: Display name
field.targetHint: OID path the rule attaches to, e.g. ITEM_BP.GROUP1.CRF1.SED1

result.heading: Rule authored
result.summary: Created 1 rule, 1 rule set, and 1 action.

import.ignoreDuplicates: Ignore duplicate rules (skip rather than overwrite)
import.parserActiveNote: The legacy XML import pipeline runs server-side: schema validation, expression validation against your study, and cycle detection. Rejected entries don't persist.

preview.summary: {validRules} new + {duplicateRules} duplicate + {invalidRules} invalid rules · {validRuleSets} new + {duplicateRuleSets} duplicate + {invalidRuleSets} invalid rule sets

sites.title: Sites
sites.intro: Create child sites under this study. Each site is a study row with its own facility metadata + role bindings; subjects + events are scoped per site. The active study must be the top-level parent — switch via the picker if you're currently on a site.
sites.createHeading: Create a site under this study
sites.empty: No sites configured under this study yet.
sites.disableConfirm: Remove site "{name}"? Existing subjects + events at this site stay in the audit trail but become inaccessible until restored. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

crfLibrary.title: CRF Library
crfLibrary.intro: Manage the form library shared across all studies in this deployment. Upload .xls / .xlsx spreadsheets to create new versions; attach versions to event definitions on the Events surface.
crfLibrary.parserActiveNote: Uploaded spreadsheets are parsed server-side. Items, sections, response sets, and version metadata are extracted on submit; rejected rows / cells surface as field errors below the file picker.
crfLibrary.parseErrorsHeading: The parser rejected the spreadsheet ({count} issue(s)):
crfLibrary.uploadIntro: Choose a .xls / .xlsx file. Give the version a short name (e.g. v1.0, v1.1). The revision notes field captures what changed since the previous version.
crfLibrary.disableConfirm: Remove CRF "{name}"? Active assignments stay intact for audit but new data entry is blocked. The action is recorded in the audit trail.
crfLibrary.disableVersionConfirm: Remove version "{version}" of "{name}"? Subjects currently entering data against this version are unaffected; new data entry will use the remaining versions.
crfLibrary.empty: No CRFs in the library yet.

author.heading: Author new version of "{name}"
author.subheading: Phase E.6 Milestone A — vertical slice (one section, two-three items, TEXT response).
author.scopeNote: Milestone A scope: ST / INTEGER / BL items with a single TEXT response set. Show-when, calculations, multi-language labels and reusable response sets land in Milestones B/C/D.
author.itemsEmpty: No items yet — add at least one to enable submit.

review.heading: Review and submit
review.intro: On submit the server synthesises an XLS workbook from this draft and runs it through the legacy CRF parser. The resulting version is semantically identical to one uploaded via the upload-version flow.
review.incomplete: Some required fields are still blank — go back and fill them in to enable submit.
review.parseErrors: The parser rejected the synthesised workbook ({count} issue(s)):

assignCrfs.title: Manage CRF assignments
assignCrfs.empty: No CRFs attached yet. Attach the first one from the library below.
assignCrfs.noVersionsNote: This CRF has no uploaded versions yet. Go to the CRF library and upload a .xls/.xlsx version first.
assignCrfs.noEligibleCrfsNote: Every CRF in the library is already attached. Create more CRFs in the library to attach more.
assignCrfs.removeConfirm: Detach CRF "{crf}" from event "{event}"? Existing data on this CRF stays in the audit trail; new entries against this event will no longer include the CRF. The action is recorded in the audit trail.
assignCrfs.auditNote: All attaches, role changes and detaches are recorded in the audit trail.

eventDefinitions.title: Event definitions
eventDefinitions.intro: Define the visit schedule for this study. Each event represents a scheduled visit, an unscheduled add-hoc visit, or a common event applied to every subject. CRF assignments live alongside in the CRF library.
eventDefinitions.repeatingLabel: Repeating event (can occur multiple times per subject)
eventDefinitions.disableConfirm: Remove event "{name}"? Existing CRFs scheduled against it remain in the audit trail but become inaccessible. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

studyForm.name: Study name
studyForm.uniqueProtocolIdHint: Short identifier used in every URL. Letters, digits, underscores, dashes. Cannot be changed after creation.

create.title: Create a new study
create.intro: Defines the protocol identity. You'll be enrolled as Coordinator on the new study and can configure events, CRFs, and sites from its dashboard. Sysadmin only.

edit.title: Edit study identity
edit.intro: Fields marked with an asterisk are required. Every change emits an audit-trail entry recording the old and new values.
edit.saved: Saved. Changes are reflected throughout the platform.

manageUsers.title: Manage Users
manageUsers.searchPlaceholder: Find user by name, username or email…
manageUsers.empty: No users match the current filter.

invite.title: Invite a new user
invite.studyMissing: Pick an active study before inviting a user.
invite.emailNotWiredNote: Welcome email delivery is not yet wired — copy the one-time password and share it with the new user via a secure channel.
invite.ssoNotWiredNote: No password is generated. After submit, the user signs in via the institutional SSO portal — make sure their institutional identity is provisioned at the IdP.
invite.successIntro: User created. Share this one-time password with the new user; they will be required to change it on first login.
invite.ssoSuccessIntro: User created. They will sign in via the institutional SSO portal — no password is needed.
invite.ssoSuccessHint: Make sure their institutional identity is provisioned at the IdP before they attempt to sign in.
invite.firstLoginNote: The password is shown once and is not stored. Make sure it is captured before closing this dialog.

authMethod.label: Authentication method
authMethod.localHelp: A one-time password will be generated and shown after the user is created.
authMethod.ssoHelp: The user signs in through the institutional identity provider. No password is generated.

invite.externalIdHelp: Case-sensitive. Must match the principal the IdP sends in the eduPersonPrincipalName header.

edit.title: Edit user profile
edit.roleNote: Manage role bindings from the Roles dialog.

lifecycle.disableConfirm: Disable user {username}? They will be unable to log in. Their study-role bindings are soft-deleted alongside; re-enabling restores them. The action is recorded in the audit trail.
lifecycle.restoreConfirm: Re-enable user {username}? A new one-time password is generated; they must change it on first login. The action is recorded in the audit trail.
lifecycle.restoredIntro: User {username} is re-enabled. Share the one-time password via a secure channel.
lifecycle.restoredNoPassword: The credential is owned by the directory or identity provider — no local password was generated. Tell the user they may log in with their existing credentials.

resetPassword.confirm: Reset {username}'s password? A new one-time password is generated; they must change it on first login. The current password is invalidated immediately. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

roles.title: Manage role bindings
roles.empty: No active role bindings on visible studies.
roles.revokeConfirm: Revoke {username}'s role on study {study}? Their access to that study is removed; their data on the study is unaffected. The action is recorded in the audit trail.
roles.auditNote: All grants, role changes, and revocations are recorded in the audit trail.

importCrf.title: Import CRF Data
importCrf.intro: Bulk-load completed CRFs from CDISC ODM XML. The import is staged — nothing is committed until you confirm at the final step.

upload.heading: Upload CDISC ODM 1.3 XML
upload.description: Subject / event / CRF / item must already be defined in the study.

map.heading: Detected counts
map.description: The file was parsed and auto-mapped against the study's structure. Resolve any conflicts in the next step.

preview.errorUnknownItem: Unknown item OID in the current CRF version
preview.warnRange: Out-of-range value — will import + flag for SDV
preview.reasonHint: {count} overwrites require a Reason for Change note. Recorded against every overwritten item in the audit log.
preview.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Site sent corrected ODM after on-site source-data reconciliation.

signSubject.title: Sign Subject {id}
signSubject.intro: Signing attests that the data is complete and accurate to the best of your knowledge. The action is recorded in the audit log and cannot be reversed without an administrator reset.
signSubject.signedToast: {id} signed. Opening the subject detail page…

error.passwordRequired: Password is required to record the e-signature.
error.badPassword: Password does not match — signature not recorded.
error.alreadySigned: Subject is already signed — signing is a one-way action.
error.preflightBlocked: Pre-flight checks block this sign action — resolve the failed checks first.
error.unknown: Could not record the signature — please try again.

signSubject.attestation: By signing below, I, {name} (Investigator, site {site}), attest that to the best of my knowledge the data captured for subject {id} is complete, accurate, and reflects the source documents. This electronic signature is recorded in the regulatory audit log per ICH-GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 §11.50.
signSubject.acknowledgement: I understand that signing locks all CRFs against further edit by site personnel until an administrator reset.

preflight.eventsCompleteTitle: All scheduled events have at least one CRF completed
preflight.eventsCompleteDetail: Every event has data — see the casebook below for the per-event status.
preflight.eventsIncompleteTitle: Some events still have CRFs in progress
preflight.eventsIncompleteDetail: Signing is allowed; CRFs in progress will be locked at their current state.
preflight.crfsCompleteTitle: All required CRFs are marked complete
preflight.crfsIncompleteTitle: Some required CRFs are not marked complete
preflight.openQueriesTitle: {count} open discrepancies attached to this subject
preflight.openQueriesDetail: Open queries do not block signing, but you should reconcile them first when possible.
preflight.noOpenQueriesTitle: No open discrepancies on this subject
preflight.firstSignature: No previous signature on this subject
preflight.alreadySigned: Subject already signed — re-signature recorded as a new audit event

login.brandLine: Department of Ophthalmology and Optometry · Medical University of Vienna
login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Use your institutional credentials.
login.delegatedHint: 2FA, password resets, and account lockout are handled by your identity provider.
login.orLocal: Or sign in with a local account
login.localAccountSummary: Sign in with a local LibreClinica account
login.localPurpose: For sponsor monitors, external personnel, and break-glass accounts that don't have an institutional identity.
login.complianceTell: This is a GCP / 21 CFR Part 11 compliant clinical-data system. All actions are audited.

firstLogin.title: Welcome, {name}
firstLogin.intro: A few details to confirm before you reach the application. 2FA, password lifecycle, and account lockout are managed by your identity provider; the fields below are LibreClinica-local.

helper.displayName: Shown in the audit log, sign-off attestations, and discrepancy threads.

firstLogin.eSignatureExplain: Clinical sign-offs (Sign Subject, Sign Event) re-authenticate you through your identity provider with forceAuthn=true per 21 CFR Part 11 §11.50.
firstLogin.eSignatureAck: I understand that re-authenticating via the identity provider is the binding signature event for clinical attestations.

terms.heading: Terms of use
terms.body1: I understand that LibreClinica MUW is a clinical-data system under ICH-GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10. Every action I take is recorded in an immutable audit log.
terms.body2: I will not share my credentials, will sign off the system when I leave my workstation, and will protect any subject identifiers visible on screen.
terms.body3: I understand that my role grants access only to data and actions consistent with my study assignment; access outside that scope is flagged in the audit log and reviewed.
terms.ackTerms: I have read and accept the terms above.
terms.ackAuditing: I understand that every action I take is recorded and auditable.

errors.saveFailed: Could not save your profile. Please try again.

changePassword.title: Change your password
changePassword.subtitle: Signed in as {username}.

firstLogin.title: Welcome — please set a new password
firstLogin.body: You are signing in for the first time. Set a password you have not used here before, then continue to LibreClinica.

rotation.title: Your password has expired
rotation.body: Your password is older than the rotation policy permits. Please set a new password to continue.

field.newPasswordHelper: Use a strong, unique password — your administrator may enforce minimum length and character-class rules.

changePassword.complianceTell: Password changes are recorded in the audit log per 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10.

errors.saveFailed: Could not change your password. Please try again.
errors.repeatMismatch: The two new-password fields do not match.

studyPicker.title: Choose a study
studyPicker.subtitle: Welcome, {name}. Pick the study you want to work on. You can switch later from the top bar.
studyPicker.empty: You do not have a role on any study yet. Ask the Data Manager to grant you access.

subjectDetail.notFound: Subject {id} not found in the current study.

actions.removeConfirm: Remove subject {id}? Events, CRFs and items will be marked deleted (soft-delete). A Data Manager can restore. The action is recorded in the audit trail.
actions.lockConfirm: Lock subject {id}? Edits and data entry will be frozen until the Data Manager unlocks. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

edit.yearOfBirthInvalid: Year of birth must be a number.

event.dateInvalid: Date must be in YYYY-MM-DD format.
event.cancelConfirm: Cancel event "{label}"? Associated CRFs and items will be marked deleted. The action is recorded in the audit trail.

scheduleEvent.title: Schedule event
scheduleEvent.subtitle: Subject {id}
scheduleEvent.empty: No event definitions are available for this study.

error.dateInvalid: Date must be in YYYY-MM-DD format.
error.generic: Could not schedule the event. Please try again.
